examples.title: Environment Class Template
examples.description: A base class template for defining environments in AI systems, demonstrating how properties can be implemented environment.
examples.explanation: This base class provides a template for implementing different types of environments. Subclasses can override the property methods to define specific environment characteristics, and implement step() and reset() for the agent-environment interaction loop.

examples.title: Chess Environment Implementation
examples.description: Implementation of a chess environment demonstrating fully observable, deterministic, discrete, static, multi-agent, and adversarial properties.
examples.explanation: This class demonstrates how to implement an environment with specific properties. Chess is fully observable (complete state available), deterministic (each move has exact consequences), discrete (finite positions), static (only changes on moves), multi-agent (two players), and adversarial (opponents compete).

examples.title: Partially Observable Environment Simulation
examples.description: Simulating a partially observable, stochastic, dynamic environment like a self-driving car scenario.
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates a partially observable, stochastic, dynamic, continuous environment. The agent can only perceive objects within sensor range, other cars move randomly (stochastic), the environment changes continuously, and position/velocity are continuous values. This mirrors real-world challenges in autonomous driving.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of other cars
